childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

node.textContent: Fix a real bug in slidey

kitsoki's bug-fix pipeline, pointed at the real slidey repo — a Node/Vue narrated-deck renderer. We'll fix a grid-cards narration-timing drift end to end: reproduce → propose → implement → test → review → validate → done. Every room is a deterministic state-machine step kitsoki runs the same way every time — this whole walk is replayed from a real fix run, no LLM in the loop now.

This card is the bug-fix pipeline, bound to an external checkout — the slidey Node repo — so the agent reads and edits real renderer code in an isolated git worktree, and CI runs the project's real node --test suite. The ticket is slidey-128. Let's run it.
Click New session to create a fresh, independently-traced run of the bug-fix pipeline against slidey issue 128.
↳ Click the highlighted control to continue.

We're on the drive view: each operator accept advances the pipeline exactly one room, and the state badge tracks where we are. The ticket — slidey-128, grid `cards` scenes with more than six items desync their narration — is already loaded. Let's reproduce it.

bf.reproducing — the agent confirms the bug is real before touching code. The frame estimator and the renderer must produce identical frame totals (a documented lock-step contract). For a grid cards scene the estimator sums `cards_item_<i> ?? 30` over every card, but the table only keys items 0–5; the renderer holds `?? 20`. A failing unit test on an 8-item grid shows the 20-frame overcount: estimate 484 vs render 464. Accept to move on.

node.textContent: Phase 1 / 7  ·  verify the bug; produce reproduction artifact
slidey-128 · cycle 0 · full bugfix · judge human
slidey-128 — Grid `cards` scenes with more than 6 items desync narration (estimate vs render drift)
slidey-128 CONFIRMED: grid cards estimator over-counts items beyond cards_item_5 by 10 frames each

## Bug Verified: slidey-128 — grid `cards` scenes with >6 items desync narration

### What is broken

The frame estimator (`estimateScene` in `src/timing.js`) and the real renderer (`src/renderer.js`) must produce the **same** frame total per scene — `timing.js`'s own NOTE says they must stay in lock-step, because narration audio is laid against the estimated boundaries.

For a grid `cards` scene the estimator sums `T['cards_item_'+i] ?? 30` over every card (`src/timing.js:330`). The TIMING table only defines `cards_item_0` … `cards_item_5`. So for the 7th card onward the estimator uses the **30**-frame fallback.

The renderer, however, holds `TIMING[stepName] ?? 20` per reveal step (`src/renderer.js:129`) — a **20**-frame generic fallback. For every card beyond `cards_item_5` the estimate is **10 frames too long**, so a deck with an 8-item grid drifts 20 frames (~0.67 s @ 30 fps) and the narration slips out of sync.

### Root cause location

`src/timing.js`, line 330:

```js
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) f += T[`cards_item_${i}`] ?? 30;
```

The `?? 30` fallback disagrees with the renderer's `?? 20`.

### How it was reproduced

A deterministic unit test was added to `test/timing.test.js` that computes the renderer-true total (one `cards_item_<i>` reveal per card, each `TIMING['cards_item_'+i] ?? 20`) for an 8-item grid and asserts `estimateScene` equals it. It fails:

```
not ok 11 - cards grid item reveals match the renderer per-item hold beyond the table
  expected: 464
  actual:   484
```

The 20-frame gap is exactly the two over-counted cards (`cards_item_6`, `cards_item_7`) at 10 frames each.

node.textContent: Phase 2 / 7  ·  draft the fix proposal for review
slidey-128 · cycle 0 · full bugfix · judge human
slidey-128 — Grid `cards` scenes with more than 6 items desync narration (estimate vs render drift)
Fix slidey-128: align grid cards estimator fallback (30→20) with the renderer per-item hold

## Bug

Grid `cards` scenes with more than 6 items desync their narration: the frame estimator over-counts each card beyond `cards_item_5` by 10 frames (uses a 30-frame fallback where the renderer holds 20).

## Root Cause

`src/timing.js:330` — `T['cards_item_'+i] ?? 30` disagrees with the renderer's `TIMING[stepName] ?? 20` (`src/renderer.js:129`) for items the TIMING table doesn't key (cards_item_6+).

## Fix

Change the estimator's grid-cards fallback from `?? 30` to `?? 20` so it matches the renderer for any card count. Add a regression test computing the renderer-true total for an 8-item grid.

## Files

- `src/timing.js` — one-line fallback change + comment
- `test/timing.test.js` — regression test

## Confidence

0.95 — the disagreement is unambiguous and the fix is the minimal reconciliation of two fallbacks that share a documented lock-step contract.

The change is committed to the fix/cards-item-timing-drift branch. Accept to run the tests.

node.textContent: Phase 3 / 7  ·  apply the proposed fix to the workspace
slidey-128 · cycle 0 · full bugfix · judge human
slidey-128 — Grid `cards` scenes with more than 6 items desync narration (estimate vs render drift)
slidey-128: align grid cards estimator fallback (30→20) with the renderer per-item hold

## Changes applied

### 1. `src/timing.js` — align the grid-cards overflow fallback (line 330)

Changed the estimator's per-item fallback from `?? 30` to `?? 20` so items beyond `cards_item_5` are estimated with the **same** 20-frame generic hold the renderer's `setState` uses (`src/renderer.js:129`). A comment documents the lock-step contract.

### 2. `test/timing.test.js` — regression test

Added `cards grid item reveals match the renderer per-item hold beyond the table`: for an 8-item grid it computes the renderer-true total and asserts `estimateScene` equals it. RED before the fix (484 vs 464), GREEN after.

The minimal fix touches exactly the one disagreeing fallback; in-table grids (≤6 cards) and all other scene types are untouched.
Fix applied — Fix slidey-128: align grid cards estimator fallback (30→20) with the renderer per-item hold

## Tests

**File:** `test/timing.test.js`

| Test | Role |
|---|---|
| `cards grid item reveals match the renderer per-item hold beyond the table` | Primary regression — an 8-item grid; asserts `estimateScene` equals the renderer-true total (one `cards_item_<i>` reveal per card, each `TIMING['cards_item_'+i] ?? 20`). RED before the fix (484 vs 464), GREEN after. |
| `cards grid variant reveals one item per card` (pre-existing) | Guards the ≤6-item path — unchanged by the fix. |

## Test run

Command: `node --test test/timing.test.js`

```
# tests 19
# pass 19
# fail 0
```

Full suite: `node --test` → 94 passed, 0 failed, 3 skipped (pre-existing).

slidey-128 fix validated — 94/94 tests pass, regression green

## Test run

`node --test` (whole suite)

```
# tests 97
# pass 94
# fail 0
# skipped 3
```

All non-skipped tests pass; the 3 skips are pre-existing (unrelated env-gated cases). The targeted timing suite is 19/19 green, including the new regression.

## slidey-128 regression

| Test | Result |
|---|---|
| `cards grid item reveals match the renderer per-item hold beyond the table` | ✅ ok |
| `cards grid variant reveals one item per card` | ✅ ok |

The primary regression confirms `estimateScene` now matches the renderer's per-item hold (`?? 20`) for grids beyond `cards_item_5`, so narration no longer drifts on >6-item grid cards scenes.

## Conclusion

The fix is complete, correct, and scoped to one estimator fallback; no regressions surfaced in the full suite.

node.textContent: Phase 7 / 7  ·  close-out artifact, hand off to pr-refinement
slidey-128 · cycle 0 · full bugfix · judge human
slidey-128 — Grid `cards` scenes with more than 6 items desync narration (estimate vs render drift)
slidey-128: grid cards narration drift fixed via estimator/renderer fallback alignment

## Bug

slidey lays narration audio against the per-scene frame boundaries produced by `estimateScene` (`src/timing.js`). For a grid `cards` scene the estimator sums `T['cards_item_'+i] ?? 30` over every card, but the TIMING table only defines `cards_item_0`…`cards_item_5`. The renderer holds `TIMING[stepName] ?? 20` (`src/renderer.js:129`) for those same un-keyed reveals. So every card beyond the 6th was estimated 10 frames too long, and a deck with a 7+ item grid drifted its narration out of sync.

## Fix

Commit `19c8798` changes the estimator's grid-cards fallback from `?? 30` to `?? 20`, matching the renderer for any card count. In-table grids (≤6 cards) and every other scene type are untouched.

## Test coverage added

- **`cards grid item reveals match the renderer per-item hold beyond the table`** — primary regression: for an 8-item grid, asserts `estimateScene` equals the renderer-true total (one `cards_item_<i>` reveal per card, each `?? 20`). RED before (484 vs 464), GREEN after.

## Outcome

Whole suite: 94 passed, 0 failed, 3 skipped (pre-existing). The targeted timing suite is 19/19. Zero refinement cycles — correct on the first attempt.
